summary: Tested two new features: (1) Unit price calculations rounded to nearest 10ths place (1 decimal) in backend and frontend, (2) Sub-category reordering via up/down arrow buttons in MANAGE tab with persistence via batch-sort-order API. All 19 backend tests passed. All 5 tabs (COUNT, INVENTORY, ORDERS, MANAGE, ACCOUNTING) working correctly.
passed_tests: Unit price rounding (backend): POST /api/items auto-calculates cost_per_case rounded to 1 decimal (44.33 * 12 = 532.0)
Unit price rounding (backend): PUT /api/items recalculates cost_per_case on cost_per_unit update (10.55 * 24 = 253.2)
Unit price rounding (backend): Rounding works for various values (27.08 * 24 = 649.9)
Batch sort order API: PUT /api/items/batch-sort-order endpoint exists and accepts batch updates
Batch sort order API: Sort order changes persist in database after batch update
Sort order field: Items have sort_order integer field, can create items with specific sort_order
Sub-category reordering UI: MANAGE tab shows up/down ChevronUp/ChevronDown arrow buttons next to sub-category headers
Sub-category reordering UI: Clicking down arrow updates sort_order and reorders items in view
Sub-category grouping: INVENTORY tab displays 'Beer - Alcopop' as separate section using sort_order
COUNT tab: Stock counting interface working with 4 location inputs (Bar, Beer, Lobby, Storage)
INVENTORY tab: Table displays all 7 columns (Item, Have, Cases, Target, Need, Order, Vendor)
ORDERS tab: Orders grouped by 3 suppliers (Singha99, Vendor, Makro) with Copy and Confirm buttons
MANAGE tab: Inline editing table with 11 columns including $/Unit, $/Case, Sale $, Target, Case?
ACCOUNTING tab: Shows 18 saved sessions, View button opens dialog with count data
All existing backend tests pass (12 original + 7 new feature tests = 19 total)
critical_code_review_comments: Backend batch-sort-order endpoint correctly placed BEFORE /items/{item_id} routes to avoid route conflict - good fix
Frontend saveItem correctly calculates costs with Math.round(value * 10) / 10 for 1 decimal rounding
Backend create_item and update_item use round(value, 1) for consistent 1 decimal rounding
groupItems function now sorts by sort_order field, enabling sub-category reordering persistence
seed_data_creation: Test items created and cleaned up during pytest runs (TEST_ prefix)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two new features verified working: (1) Unit price rounding to 1 decimal place implemented in both backend (server.py create_item/update_item) and frontend (App.js saveItem). (2) Sub-category reordering via ChevronUp/ChevronDown buttons in MANAGE tab - updates sort_order field via PUT /api/items/batch-sort-order endpoint. Order persists across all tabs via groupItems function sorting by sort_order. App has 81 items, 18 sessions. Test file test_new_features.py added with 7 tests for new features.
